title: Customer Insight & Adoption Lead (f/m/d)
required_skills: Proven experience in customer insight, user research, or product discovery
Translating customer research into actionable insights for product/UX teams
Working in agile product-led environment with PMs and designers
Synthesising qualitative feedback into clear themes
bonus_skills: Experience running customer councils, advisory boards, or early adopter programmes
UX research or service design background
Usage data / adoption analytics alongside qualitative insight
matched_skills: Customer insight and analytics: Hapag-Lloyd — analysed customer journey and user research data, translated into executive sprint review reports
Agile product-led environment: Hapag-Lloyd sprint ceremonies, cross-functional coordination
Stakeholder communication: HSBC C-suite reporting, structured narratives
KPI reporting and performance analytics: multiple roles
German B1 (partial — role requires fluent German)
missing_skills: Formal user research / UX research methodology
Fluent German (Boru B1 vs required fluent)
core_responsibilities: Own collection, synthesis, and communication of customer/user insights
Work directly with customers to understand needs, workflows, and pain points
Plan and conduct qualitative and quantitative research activities
Translate insights into actionable inputs for Product Managers and UX designers
Own and manage customer councils and early adopter programmes
Monitor feature adoption post-release and feed back into product cycles
recommended_emphasis: Hapag-Lloyd customer journey and user research data analysis — most direct match to insight collection role
Structured executive reporting across Hapag-Lloyd and HSBC — demonstrates synthesis and communication capability
IPC Marketing B2B client management as customer engagement analog
GRG trade fair client engagement (100+ B2B clients) as customer-facing research experience
summary: Score 43.8 (weak match). Boru's closest alignment is at Hapag-Lloyd, where customer journey analysis and research synthesis were performed. However, this role requires formal UX research expertise, customer council management, and fluent German — all of which are gaps. The role is fundamentally a user researcher / product discovery specialist rather than a PM or PMO. Proceed only if Boru is interested in pivoting toward UX research and can demonstrate the German language gap is being addressed.
